item.title: Shy Allosaurus Music Dump, Disc 5
item.description:
Crazy how nature do that.
How vinyl albums are made. That's where the term "cutting a record" came from. TMYK
Psychosalad
Μῆνιν ἄειδε θεὰ Πηληϊάδεω Ἀχιλῆος (Sing to me, Muse, of Achilles, the son of Peleus)
I ain't got no crystal ball.
"Silver" by Russ Leonard https://youtu.be/arnDb_InOfQ?si=CNQsZrVDtpRNxNEp
Stephen Fry GOAT narrator.

item.dc:creator: Nexperyin

item.title: A little transition update ?️‍⚧️
item.description: Yesterday was a whirlwind. After decades of having the wrong equipment, I finally got my bottom surgery! Two years of therapy and HRT paid off and my body finally matches my gender identity. I'm SO happy despite still having a catheter inside me while I post this from my hospital bed. Thought about posting a selfie but wasn't sure if even this life event justifies it among my fellow Imgurians. Life is so incredible!!
item.dc:creator: lurker2112

item.title: I’m a crafty bitch
item.description:
Made me some new shoes for dragoncon this year! This was my first time doing something like this so there was a lot of trial and error in my process. They aren’t perfect but I’m super happy with them!
Found some $4 boots at Aldi. Plan was hatched. Also hot tip for small-footed people. A woman’s 6 is a children’s 4. Children’s shoes are easily found up to size 5.
My first step was to try to make a pattern using the a common cosplay method. This failed and the duct tape kept moving around. It wasn’t until later I learned I probably could have made a pattern with the painters tape that would have led to fewer scraps than the process I ended up using made.
Look at my failure.
Taped them up and then painted them for a more neutral base
Fabric cutting time! I’m not using most of this
I cut around a foot of a six foot long fabric
More paint
Attached my fabric with rubber cement. Started with the tongue of the shoe
It lives!
Finished with a fabric mod podge
Sealed with this mod podge to make it outdoor safe
Painted some fuck up spots with some blue acrylic paint
And the finished look!
Beautiful
Taxes
item.dc:creator: dontmakemechooseaname

item.title: Smol dump of legally stolen memes and things
item.description:
Hello
She likes it.
Yum.
I quite enjoy saying unsettling things.
Except when I remember them at 3AM
Doubts, fears, insatiable desires?
NGL I get catfished by produce the most.
Some people need a warning, I guess.
Pretty sure they're even, if such a thing was measured.
Absolute clown shoes
This would be amazing.
They're so annoying.
Wait till they get to the ;
Captcha - because now we are quizzed for life.
It got me in the feels.
Like romantically?
Does anyone really lose here?
I've played with many genres, but my newest one would be the most offensive. It's a lot of fun.
Including writing random thoughts on a website for meaningless points.
For real
This is for the trash panda that sends me trash pics. You know who you are.
Cooper tax

item.title: There are people whom I admire but do not want to meet, because I'd be embarrassed at my inability to hold up my end of a serious conversation with them. Dr. May is one of them.
item.dc:creator: Newitt

item.title: Wednesday WHOLESOME dump to help you with your mental health
item.description:
1. Bros just added 20 years to their lives
2
3. "And then there's Stella" :D
4. Do you think birds experience happiness?
5. Such a cute kid+fish combo :)
6. This dog playing volleball
7. I love when people make random strangers laugh
8. JUST DANCE
9. When you finally scratch that itchy butt
10. Dog having a great day at the water park
11. I scary
12
13
14. Imagine driving trough this stunning place (kinda scary but also awesome)
15. Thank you for making it all the way down! I feel like writing today and would like to share a little bit about a passion project I'm working on and why this means so much to me. Do Not Press The Button is more than just a game to me—it's a labor of love, born out of countless hours of passion, creativity, and dedication. When I first started working on this project, my goal was to create something that would not only entertain but also resonate with players on a deeper level. It started as a goofy idea, but now, almost at the finish line, I believe I've managed to give it heart, soul, and spirit. As an independent developer, the journey has been both incredibly rewarding and challenging. I've poured everything I've got into this project, and now, I'm at a point where I need your support to bring this dream fully to life. I believe in Do Not Press The Button with all my heart, and I truly hope it will bring as much joy to you as it has brought me while creating it. Your support—whether through wishlisting, sharing, or simply cheering from the sidelines—would mean the world to me. Thank you for considering this and for being part of this journey. I couldn’t do this without you. https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. HAVE A GREAT AND SUNNY WEEK, FRIENDOS!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Earlier this month, a great scientist passed away
item.description: https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-02585-1 Outside the Institute of High Energy Physics in Beijing stands a 5-metre-high metal sculpture. The round, swirling shape of ‘The Tao of All Matter’ invokes the yin and yang of ancient Chinese philosophy — a representation of the inseparable opposites said to form the basis of all things — as well as the ring of the modern particle accelerator a few dozen metres away. The designer was physicist Tsung-Dao (T. D.) Lee. An artistic, inventive and boundary-breaking physicist, in 1957 Lee became one of the youngest winners of a Nobel prize at 30 years old. He shared the award with his compatriot Chen Ning Yang for their work on broken symmetry in particle physics. Chinese-born but based in the United States for most of his career, Lee, who has died aged 97, promoted engagement between US and Chinese physicists. After China’s Cultural Revolution (1966–76), it was difficult for US universities to evaluate the abilities of Chinese applicants, because they did not know the assessment criteria used in China. Lee set up the China-US Physics Examination and Application system, through which US physicists set exams for Chinese universities to administer, and then went to China to mark them. On the basis of the results, US universities awarded scholarships to nearly 1,000 Chinese students between 1979 and 1989. Lee’s own education in Shanghai had been interrupted by the 1937 Japanese invasion of China, and by 1945 he was studying physics at the National Southwestern Associated University in Kunming, China. There, at 19 years old, he received a fellowship from the Chinese government to study in the United States, where he completed a PhD at the University of Chicago, Illinois, with the Italian American nuclear physicist and Nobel prizewinner Enrico Fermi as his adviser. He spent the rest of his career at Columbia University in New York City. Lee earned his own Nobel in one of the most remarkable episodes of twentieth-century science. Physicists were baffled by two particles, known as τ and θ, that were identical except that they decayed in different ways. Treating them as distinct particles created insoluble theoretical problems, whereas treating them as identical defied the fundamental structure of physics known as parity — the idea that objects always behave in the same way as their mirror images. In 1956, Lee worked on the problem with Yang while the two were spending the summer at Brookhaven National Laboratory in Upton, New York. Colleagues remember hearing them shouting at each other in their offices in Chinese and English, and drawing equations in the sand on visits to the beach. Did physicists, the two asked, really know that parity structured the ‘weak interaction’ that governed decays of τ and θ? After wading through a 1,000-page book detailing 40 years of work on the subject, they realized that that aspect of parity had not been tested. On 22 June 1956, Lee and Yang sent an article to Physical Review with the title ‘Is parity conserved in weak interactions?’. The journal’s editor insisted that question marks in paper titles were unscientific and changed it to ‘Question of parity conservation in weak interactions’ (T. D. Lee and C. N. Yang Phys. Rev. 104, 254; 1956). Lee and Yang struggled to interest experimenters in testing a hypothesis widely regarded as preposterous. Only Chien-Shiung Wu at Columbia agreed. She set up an experiment to detect β-decay — the emission of β-particles from nuclei — in ultracold radioactive cobalt. At the tail end of 1956, her team announced that parity was indeed violated in the weak interaction. As another Columbia colleague, Nobel-prizewinning physicist Isidor Rabi, said at a press conference: “A rather complete theoretical structure has been shattered at the base and we are not sure how the pieces will be put together.” By the end of the year, Lee and Yang had been jointly awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics for their discovery. Lee and others felt that Wu deserved a share of the prize, but she never received it. Lee and Yang’s personal relationship did not survive the success of their professional one. To their colleagues’ distress and incomprehension, their clashes became so fierce that universities and laboratories took care to invite only one at a time for visits. Lee was influential in the evolution of the Brookhaven lab when it faced an uncertain future after a huge high-energy accelerator project was cancelled in 1983. He helped the lab to transform that project into another, the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC), which would study the nature of nuclear matter. Lee then established and was first director of the RIKEN BNL Research Center, a pioneering collaboration between Japanese and US scientists at the RHIC. As an assistant professor at Columbia University in the early 1950s, Lee initiated ‘Chinese lunch’, a tradition in which, at noon on Mondays, physicists would take a visiting seminar speaker to a local Chinese restaurant. Lee insisted on ordering for everyone, and the conversation was usually so lively that participants would have to race back to campus in time for the seminar to begin at 14:10. His research contributed to an astounding variety of fields, including astrophysics, hydrodynamics, particle physics, condensed-matter physics, relativistic physics and statistical mechanics. Halfway around the world from Beijing, at the Santa Maria degli Angeli basilica in Rome, is another of Lee’s sculptures: ‘Galileo Galilei Divine Man’. In it, the revolutionary seventeenth-century astronomer, philosopher, physicist and artist holds a telescope and robes adorned with heavenly bodies. Both sculpted and sculptor were scientists whose work ventured across boundaries: between scientific fields and between the sciences and the arts.

item.title: When you see someone using a low-quality version of a GIF that has an Intergalactic Quality Upgrade available.
item.description: Source: Deadpool & Wolverine (2024) #intergalacticqualitygifs (Check WiFi) Good news, everyone! The Intergalactic Quality Gifs tag has now surpassed 12,500 followers! Thanks for all the support and love. This community is without a doubt the best!
item.dc:creator: BabyFarkMcGeeZax

item.title: Stolen thangs for ya.
item.dc:creator: sleepycity

item.title: Thank you, @TinyOctopus!
item.description: So the legend known as @TinyOctopus asked if he could send a gift to Jasmne in the form of the catnip called CatCrack. Of course I said yes!! I busted it out tonight with high hopes. But…I’ve heard that younger kittens don’t always react to catnip, and that seemed to hold true with Jasmne. However, my cat, Scooty Booty, was enamored! Her little hops up to smell it were so cute. You may not have gotten Jasmne to go crazy, but Scoots rolled around in it and ate it up like a madwoman! Thank you!! I’ll try it again with my future foster litters and share the results!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Mostly old meme dump. Mostly appropriated from here.
item.description: Birb Tax. Paco likes string cheese, he actually likes all cheese. He will fight anyone for cheese. I suggest if you meet Paco you willingly give him the cheese. His face knife is really sharp. Don’t tell me or Paco that birds shouldn’t have cheese, we don’t care. We loves the cheese!
item.dc:creator: Cat2BKittenMe
